data.title: Fibocom unveils Power Class 1 (PC1) modules at MWC 2026, based on MediaTek T930 and Qualcomm X85/X82 platforms. Major breakthrough in high-power 5G module tech.
data.description: On March 4, 2026, in Barcelona, Fibocom unveiled a groundbreaking development at MWC 2026. They introduced the first-ever Power Class 1 (PC1) modules utilizing the MediaTek T930 and Qualcomm X85/X82 platforms. This signifies a significant advancement in high-power 5G module technology. This news comes amidst the ongoing advancements in 5G technology and the increasing need for faster and more efficient connectivity solutions globally.

data.title: Prominent coaches sign letter defending universities from govt interference that harms student-athletes, putting college sports at risk.
data.description: Leading coaches and sports authorities express concern about the negative impact of government intervention on universities, averring that it is adversely affecting student-athletes and jeopardizing college sports. The group of individuals, which includes renowned current and former college basketball coaches and athletic figures such as Steve, shared their apprehension during an event in New York on March 4, 2026. This collective warning highlights the potential risks to the sports community due to federal and state meddling in university affairs.

data.title: Hilco Global appoints Ted Tomaras as COO of Professional Services. Ted brings expertise to diversified financial services company.
data.description: Hilco Global, a financial services company based in Northbrook, Illinois, disclosed on March 4, 2026, that Ted Tomaras has been appointed as the new Chief Operating Officer of its Professional division. Specializing in asset valuation, monetization, advisory, and capital solutions, Hilco Global aims to benefit from Tomaras' expertise in this role.

data.title: Blaker & Granet LLP, an LA immigration law firm, celebrates 4 years with partners Blaker and Granet founding in 2022.
data.description: In Los Angeles on March 4, 2026, Blaker & Granet LLP, an immigration law firm, reached its fourth anniversary milestone. The firm, established in 2022 by Stephen Blaker and Jacki Granet, focuses on U.S. immigration and naturalization law. This anniversary signifies four years of specialized legal services in the field.

data.title: Radial Entertainment acquires New Dominion Pictures' catalog, expanding its portfolio. Deal amount undisclosed.
data.description: On March 4, 2026, in Los Angeles, Radial Entertainment, a prominent independent distributor supported by funds managed by Oaktree Capital Management, L.P., acquired the New Dominion Pictures catalog. The undisclosed sum for this purchase strengthens Radial Entertainment's owned content portfolio significantly.
US Official: US defense executives to visit the White House Friday to discuss increasing weapons production - CNN.

data.title: CapNexus emerges as a tech consulting firm after acquiring Makira, offering services to enterprises and retail.
data.description: A technology consulting and managed services company, CapNexus has been launched in Middletown, New York. The company, formed through the merger of Capstone Integrated Solutions, RPE Solutions, and the acquisition of Makira, a top retail implementation and integration firm, aims to provide comprehensive technology transformation services. Operating as one entity, CapNexus will serve enterprise and retail organizations, offering a full spectrum of solutions. This strategic move brings together a wealth of expertise to cater to a wide range of clients, enhancing the technology landscape in the region.

data.title: Pivot Bio lowers prices, boosts production to aid farmers facing high global nitrogen costs; supporting spring planting.
data.description: An agtech company, Pivot Bio, based in Minneapolis, has recently reduced prices and boosted production of its nitrogen-fixing products to assist U.S. farmers in fulfilling their crop nutrition requirements during the spring planting season, amid surging global nitrogen prices. The company, recognized as a leading innovator in the industry, aims to support farmers facing significant challenges by offering lower prices instead of increasing them. This initiative comes at a crucial time as farmers gear up for the planting season, reflecting the company's commitment to standing with farmers during this critical period.

data.title: Emigrant Bancorp acquires Fortis Management Group to launch Emigrant Family Office, serving ultra-high-net-worth families.
data.description: Fortis is set to be the focal point of a freshly established Emigrant business segment offering exclusive non-investment family office amenities to ultra-high-net-worth families and RIAs. The entity, Emigrant Bancorp, Inc. ("Emigrant"), will oversee this development in Miami starting from March 4, 2026.

data.title: CrowdStrike reported record Q4 earnings with net-new ARR up 47% YoY to $331 million and a total ARR of $5.25 billion. Various analysts maintain ratings and price targets, reflecting growth in emerging products.
data.description: CrowdStrike Holdings Inc (CRWD) beat fourth-quarter earnings expectations, as reported on Tuesday. Analyst Kingsley Crane at Canaccord Genuity reiterated a Hold rating, lowering the price target to $400. Mike Cikos at Needham maintained a Buy rating but reduced the price target to $475. Jonathan Ruykhaver at Cantor Fitzgerald reaffirmed an Overweight rating with a price target of $520, while Catharine Trebnick at Rosenblatt Securities kept a Buy rating and $555 price target. John DiFucci at Guggenheim Securities reiterated a Neutral rating. CrowdStrike posted record quarterly results with net-new ARR up 47% YoY to $331 million, marking a significant increase from the previous quarter's $263 million. The company ended the year with an ARR of $5.25 billion, showing three consecutive quarters of accelerating net-new ARR growth. Additionally, Cloud Security ARR showed strong growth.

data.title: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises Inc. skyrockets with $2.4 billion AI data center project; will deliver 1.2 gigawatts of power.
data.description: Shares of Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises Inc. (NYSE: BW) surged after the company announced a $2.4 billion design-build agreement with Base Electron to deliver 1.2 gigawatts of new power generation capacity for AI data centers. The project includes four 300-megawatt natural gas-fired boilers and steam turbine generator systems. Base Electron, backed by Applied Digital Corp. (NASDAQ: APLD), is developing assets to provide capacity to the grid and contracted customers. Babcock & Wilcox will handle engineering, procurement, and construction, with Siemens Energy AG designing and supplying the steam turbine generator sets. B&W Chairman and CEO Kenneth Young highlighted the company's strategic role in supporting AI data centers' power needs.
Iran: Targeted more than 10 oil tanker vessels - IRIB.

data.title: TRG closes $72M financing for AGSA, a top teak operator in Latin America.
data.description: In New York, global asset manager The Rohatyn Group ("TRG") has completed a $72 million senior secured financing deal for Agropecuaria Santa Genoveva, S.A.P.I. de C.V. ("AGSA"), a leading plantation teak operator in Latin America. TRG took charge of structuring and underwriting the transaction, showcasing its expertise in Private Credit and Forestry and Agriculture sectors.

data.title: Mr. Clean returns from retirement to launch innovative product lineup, claims "there's a lot of magic" in it.
data.description: In Cincinnati today, Mr. Clean makes a comeback from retirement with a burst of inspiration and energy to introduce the biggest innovation in his renowned career. Dave Landers, Vice President of North America Surface Care at Procter & Gamble, expressed excitement for Mr. Clean's return, highlighting the magic in the new product lineup. The legendary figure is set to unveil the latest innovation, indicating that his legacy is far from over. This comeback marks a significant event with high expectations surrounding the partnership.

data.title: BCEN celebrates 20 years of ground transport nursing specialty certification, ensuring expert critical care by board-certified RNs.
data.description: In OAK BROOK, Ill., on March 4, 2026, the Board of Certification for Emergency Nursing (BCEN) is commending board-certified ground transport Registered Nurses (RNs) for their exceptional critical care expertise. These RNs provide top-tier care every second and every mile. BCEN is a leading authority for nursing specialty certification, setting the standard for quality in the industry. This recognition showcases the dedication and proficiency of these RNs who are essential in delivering crucial care during emergencies.
